name: The Health & Education
description_short: Federal credit union in Lexington, KY serving primarily healthcare workers with $119.4M in assets, 12,173 members, and 2 branch locations since 1956.
description_long:
The Health & Education Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union headquartered in Lexington, Kentucky, that has operated for 70 years since its founding in 1956. It serves a membership base of approximately 12,173 people, primarily within the healthcare industry through a multiple common bond structure. The credit union operates 2 branch locations with a team of 28 full and part-time employees and maintains total assets of $119.44 million as of September 30, 2025.

The credit union offers standard banking and financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and loan products. Their loan portfolio includes real estate loans ($23.8M), unsecured loans ($2.4M), and other loan products ($18M), totaling $44.2M in outstanding loans. The organization also provides wire transfer services, positioning itself as a low-cost option for members needing this capability.

The Health & Education FCU distinguishes itself through its focused membership model targeting healthcare professionals and its long operational history in the Lexington market. As a federal credit union, it is insured by the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund (NCUSIF), providing deposit protection up to federal limits. The credit union reported net income of $1.93M in the most recent period, indicating stable operations.

This credit union is best suited for healthcare workers and professionals seeking community-focused banking with local decision-making. However, membership eligibility is restricted to those within the healthcare industry, limiting its accessibility to the general public. The 2-branch footprint also means geographic convenience is limited to the Lexington area. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Money Market and other deposit accounts
pros: Federally insured deposits through NCUSIF providing standard deposit protection
70-year operational history demonstrating long-term stability and community presence
Focused membership model serving healthcare professionals with specialized understanding
Low-cost wire transfer services available to members
Strong capital position with $19.2M in total equity
Reasonable loan portfolio diversity across real estate, unsecured, and other loan types
Local decision-making through Lexington headquarters with identified leadership
cons: Membership restricted to healthcare industry, excluding general public access
Limited branch network with only 2 locations restricts geographic convenience
No investment products available (zero total investments reported)
Relatively small asset base compared to larger regional credit unions limits product sophistication
No FHLB membership, which may limit liquidity management options
best_for: Healthcare professionals and employees in the Lexington, Kentucky area
Workers seeking community-focused credit union banking with local oversight
Members of the healthcare industry requiring basic checking, savings, and loan services
diagnosis: The Health & Education Federal Credit Union is best for healthcare industry workers in the Lexington area seeking straightforward, federally insured banking services from a stable, locally-managed institution. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to healthcare professionals, making this inaccessible to the general public, and its 2-branch footprint limits physical access outside central Lexington.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
